source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
A wooden block with a mass of $0.5\mathrm{kg}$ slides up a smooth inclined plane at an angle of $30^{\circ}$ with a speed of $10\mathrm{m/s}$ (assuming the plane is sufficiently long), and $g$ is taken as $10\mathrm{m}/\mathrm{s}^{2}$.

1. What is the momentum of the wooden block at the end of 1s?
2. What is the change in momentum of the wooden block in 3 seconds?

image_dependency: requires_image=true and text_dominant=false indicate an intended visual component
asset_registry_record shows 1 valid image and registry_passed=true
however, the question text itself states all key quantities explicitly (mass, angle, speed, g) and does not explicitly reference a figure, so image necessity is not strongly established
multi_step: solving requires combining at least two physics steps: resolve acceleration along the incline using g*sin(30°), then use time evolution of velocity/momentum
the item has two sub-questions, including a momentum-at-time query and a change-in-momentum-over-interval query
the 3-second part also involves direction/sign handling as the block decelerates and can reverse direction
verifiability: answer_type is short_text with numeric outputs, which are straightforward to compare against computed results
the setup is fully parameterized in text (m=0.5 kg, v0=10 m/s, angle=30°, g=10 m/s^2), enabling deterministic checking
registry_passed=true and answer_present=true support that the sample is complete and checkable

decision_reasons: Core assets are present and usable: question, answer, and image all exist, so the sample should remain in the candidate pool.
The item has good collection value because it is multi-step and highly verifiable from the provided parameters.
However, there are annotation-quality risks: the momentum-change part may depend on sign convention, and the reversal after 2 seconds can introduce solution variance.
The image role is not clearly necessary from the text, so the sample is less clean as an image-grounded physics item.
